# Animated logo for SRE dashboard (2025-12-09 14:40Z)

_**User (2025-12-09 11:50Z)**_

please put this logo for my SRE dashboard:Can i put avideo or gif as logo becaus ei wnat it to look animated?

---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_



---

You can use an animated logo. Three common options:

- Short looping GIF: Easiest to drop into an `<img>` or `<Image>` component. Just keep file size small and loop cleanly.
- MP4/WebM video: Better quality and smaller size than GIF. Use a silent, looping, autoplay video (`<video muted autoplay loop playsinline>`). You’ll need hosting or to serve the file.
- Lottie JSON: Vector animation with tiny payload; great for dashboards. Needs a Lottie player/component.

Recommendations for a dashboard:
- Prefer MP4/WebM or Lottie over GIF for crispness and smaller size.
- Keep dimensions modest (e.g., 120–200px width) and file size low to avoid layout or performance issues.
- Ensure autoplay works: videos must be muted; Lottie always works; GIFs always auto-play.

If you share your stack (e.g., React/Next/Vue) and where the logo lives, I can drop in the code snippet to embed a looping video or Lottie.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Planning animated logo guidance**

I'm confirming the app uses Next.js 16, React 19, and Tailwind and locating the header with the Activity icon around lines 247–281 in `app/page.tsx`. ---

Stack details: Next.js 16 + React 19 app at `sre-dashboard` (Tailwind styles in `globals.css`, layout in `src/app/layout.tsx`). The header lives in `src/app/page.tsx`; the current logo is the `Activity` icon inside the black header card.

```247:281:sre-dashboard/src/app/page.tsx
<header className="mb-8">
  <div className="bg-black text-white p-6 border-b-8 border-yellow-400 ...">
    <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
      <div className="w-16 h-16 bg-white border-4 border-black ...">
        <Activity className="w-10 h-10 text-black animate-pulse" />
      </div>
      <div>
        <h1 className="text-4xl md:text-5xl font-black ...">
          el Agénte <span className="text-yellow-400 ...">v2.0</span>
        </h1>
```

Best animated logo options here:
- MP4/WebM clip: sharp and smaller than GIF. Drop the file in `sre-dashboard/public/animated-logo.mp4` (or `.webm`), then replace the icon block with a `<video>` tag.
- GIF: simplest drop-in, but usually larger/softer.
- Lottie: highest quality/tiny size, but would need adding a player library (not currently in deps).

Quick swap to use a video (no extra deps):
```tsx
{/* replace the inner div currently holding <Activity /> */}
<div className="w-16 h-16 bg-white border-4 border-black overflow-hidden shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_#fbbf24]">
  <video
    src="/animated-logo.mp4" // place the file in public/
    className="w-full h-full object-cover"
    autoPlay
    loop
    muted
    playsInline
  />
</div>
```

Notes:
- Keep the file small (target <1–2 MB) and loop-friendly.
- Use `.webm` alongside `.mp4` if you want wider codec coverage; set `srcSet` via `<source>` elements if desired.
- If you prefer a GIF instead, just change to `<img src="/animated-logo.gif" ... />`.

If you want a Lottie-based logo instead, I can add the lightweight player and wire it up.

} from './components/StatusBadge';\nimport { DiagnosisReport } from './components/DiagnosisReport';\nimport { PipelineFlow, PipelineState, eventsToPipelineState, initialPipelineState } from './components/PipelineFlow';\nimport { ChainOfThought, ThoughtStep, eventsToThoughtSteps } from './components/ChainOfThought';\n\nimport { DemoModeButton } from './components/DemoModeButton';\nimport { FaultPropagationChain, parseFaultPropagation } from './components/FaultPropagationChain';\nimport { ServiceDependencyGraph, parseServiceGraph } from './components/ServiceDependencyGraph';\nimport { HypothesisPanel, parseDiagnosis } from './components/HypothesisPanel';\n\nconst ORCHESTRATOR_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ORCHESTRATOR_URL || 'http://localhost:8003';\n\ninterface DiagnosisEvent {\n    event_type: string;\n    timestamp: number;\n    data: {\n        message?: string;\n        service?: string;\n        tool?: string;\n        args?: Record&lt;string, unknown&gt;;\n        preview?: string;\n        duration?: number;\n        response?: string;\n        status?: string;\n        is_error?: boolean;\n        title?: string;\n        server?: string;\n        progress?: string;\n        servers?: string[];\n        related_services?: string[];\n        [key: string]: unknown;\n    };\n}\n\nexport default function Home() {\n    const [serviceName, setServiceName] = useState('');\n    const [isDiagnosing, setIsDiagnosing] = useState(false);\n    const [report, setReport] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\n    const [runId, setRunId] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\n    const [pipelineState, setPipelineState] = useState&lt;PipelineState&gt;(initialPipelineState);\n    const [events, setEvents] = useState&lt;DiagnosisEvent[]&gt;([]);\n    const [isListening, setIsListening] = useState(false);\n    const [thoughtSteps, setThoughtSteps] = useState&lt;ThoughtStep[]&gt;([]);\n\n    const eventSourceRef = useRef&lt;EventSource | null&gt;(null);\n\n    // Topology-aware state\n    const [faultPropagation, setFaultPropagation] = useState&lt;{ impactPath: string[]; rootCauseService?: string }&gt;({ impactPath: [] });\n    const [serviceGraph, setServiceGraph] = useState&lt;{ services: string[]; edges: Array&lt;{ from: string; to: string }&gt;; healthMap: Record&lt;string, string&gt; }&gt;({\n        services: [],\n        edges: [],\n        healthMap: {}\n    });\n    const [diagnosis, setDiagnosis] = useState&lt;{ rootCause?: string; confidence?: number; evidence?: string[]; runbook?: Array&lt;{ step: number; action: string; command?: string }&gt; }&gt;({});\n\n    // Process events into thought steps and topology data\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        if (events.length &gt; 0) {\n            // Update thought steps\n            setThoughtSteps(eventsToThoughtSteps(events));\n\n            // Continuously update service graph and fault propagation from tool results\n            const serviceGraphData = parseServiceGraph(events);\n            const faultData = parseFaultPropagation(events);\n\n            console.log('[Dashboard] Events count:', events.length);\n            console.log('[Dashboard] Service Graph:', serviceGraphData);\n            console.log('[Dashboard] Fault Propagation:', faultData);\n\n            setServiceGraph(serviceGraphData);\n            setFaultPropagation(faultData);\n\n            // Parse diagnosis data from complete event\n            const completeEvent = events.find(e =&gt; e.event_type === 'complete');\n            if (completeEvent) {\n                console.log('[Dashboard] Complete event found:', completeEvent);\n                console.log('[Dashboard] Response preview:', completeEvent.data.response?.slice(0, 500));\n\n                const diagnosisData = parseDiagnosis(events);\n                console.log('[Dashboard] Parsed diagnosis:', diagnosisData);\n                setDiagnosis(diagnosisData);\n            }\n        }\n    }, [events]);\n\n\n    // Subscribe to SSE events\n    const subscribeToEvents = useCallback((newRunId: string) =&gt; {\n        if (eventSourceRef.current) {\n            eventSourceRef.current.close();\n        }\n\n        setIsListening(true);\n        const eventSource = new EventSource(&#96;${ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/events/${newRunId}&#96;);\n        eventSourceRef.current = eventSource;\n\n        eventSource.onmessage = (e) =&gt; {\n            try {\n                const event: DiagnosisEvent = JSON.parse(e.data);\n                setEvents(prev =&gt; [...prev, event]);\n\n                // Check for completion\n                if (event.event_type === 'complete') {\n                    setIsDiagnosing(false);\n                    setIsListening(false);\n                    eventSource.close();\n\n                    // Set report if we have a response\n                    if (event.data.response) {\n                        setReport(event.data.response);\n                    }\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                console.error('Failed to parse event:', err);\n            }\n        };\n\n        eventSource.onerror = () =&gt; {\n            console.error('EventSource error');\n            setIsListening(false);\n            eventSource.close();\n        };\n    }, []);\n\n    // Update pipeline state when events change\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        if (events.length &gt; 0) {\n            setPipelineState(eventsToPipelineState(events));\n        }\n    }, [events]);\n\n    // Cleanup on unmount\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        return () =&gt; {\n            if (eventSourceRef.current) {\n                eventSourceRef.current.close();\n            }\n        };\n    }, []);\n\n    // Poll for new alert-triggered runs (auto-subscribe to Grafana alerts)\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        let lastSeenRunId: string | null = null;\n\n        const pollForAlerts = async () =&gt; {\n            try {\n                const res = await fetch(&#96;${ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/latest-run&#96;);\n                if (!res.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await res.json();\n\n                // Check if there's a new run we haven't seen\n                if (\n                    data.run_id &amp;&amp;\n                    data.run_id !== lastSeenRunId &amp;&amp;\n                    data.run_id !== runId &amp;&amp;\n                    !isDiagnosing\n                ) {\n                    lastSeenRunId = data.run_id;\n\n                    // Auto-subscribe to the new alert-triggered run\n                    setRunId(data.run_id);\n                    setServiceName(data.service || '');\n                    setIsDiagnosing(true);\n                    setReport(null);\n                    setEvents([]);\n                    setThoughtSteps([]);\n\n                    setPipelineState(initialPipelineState);\n                    // Reset topology state\n                    setFaultPropagation({ impactPath: [] });\n                    setServiceGraph({ services: [], edges: [], healthMap: {} });\n                    setDiagnosis({});\n\n                    subscribeToEvents(data.run_id);\n                }\n            } catch {\n                // Silently ignore polling errors\n            }\n        };\n\n        const interval = setInterval(pollForAlerts, 3000);\n\n        // Initial poll\n        pollForAlerts();\n\n        return () =&gt; clearInterval(interval);\n    }, [runId, isDiagnosing, subscribeToEvents]);\n\n    const handleDiagnose = async (e: React.FormEvent) =&gt; {\n        e.preventDefault();\n        if (!serviceName.trim()) return;\n\n        // Reset state\n        setIsDiagnosing(true);\n        setReport(null);\n        setEvents([]);\n        setThoughtSteps([]);\n\n        setPipelineState(initialPipelineState);\n        setRunId(null);\n        // Reset topology state\n        setFaultPropagation({ impactPath: [] });\n        setServiceGraph({ services: [], edges: [], healthMap: {} });\n        setDiagnosis({});\n\n        try {\n            const response = await fetch(&#96;${ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/diagnose&#96;, {\n                method: 'POST',\n                headers: {\n                    'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',\n                },\n                body: &#96;text=${encodeURIComponent(serviceName)}&#96;,\n            });\n\n            if (!response.ok) {\n                throw new Error('Failed to start diagnosis');\n            }\n\n            const data = await response.json();\n\n            if (data.run_id) {\n                setRunId(data.run_id);\n                subscribeToEvents(data.run_id);\n            } else {\n                throw new Error('No run_id returned from server');\n            }\n\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.error('Diagnosis error:', error);\n            setIsDiagnosing(false);\n        }\n    };\n\n    return (\n        &lt;main className=\"min-h-screen bg-[#e0f2fe] font-sans overflow-x-hidden relative selection:bg-pink-400 selection:text-black\"&gt;\n            {/* Background grid */}\n            &lt;div className=\"fixed inset-0 opacity-10 pointer-events-none z-0\"\n                style={{ backgroundImage: 'linear-gradient(#000 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(90deg, #000 1px, transparent 1px)', backgroundSize: '40px 40px' }} /&gt;\n\n            &lt;div text-white p-6 border-b-8 border-yellow-400 shadow-[12px_12px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,0.2)] transform -rotate-1 hover:rotate-0 transition-transform duration-300\"&gt;\n                        &lt;div  &lt;/h3&gt;\n                    &lt;PipelineFlow state={pipelineState} /&gt;\n                &lt;/div&gt;\n\n                {/* Topology Visualization - Fault Propagation &amp; Service Map */}\n                &lt;div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-8\"&gt;\n                    &lt;FaultPropagationChain\n                        impactPath={faultPropagation.impactPath}\n                        rootCauseService={faultPropagation.rootCauseService}\n                        serviceStatuses={Object.fromEntries(\n                            serviceGraph.services.map(s =&gt; [s, serviceGraph.services.length === 0}\n                    /&gt;\n                &lt;/div&gt;\n\n                {/* Main Content Grid - Two Columns */}\n                &lt;div className=\"grid HypothesisPanel, parseDiagnosis } from './components/HypothesisPanel';\n\nconst ORCHESTRATOR_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ORCHESTRATOR_URL || 'http://localhost:8003';\n\ninterface DiagnosisEvent {\n    event_type: string;\n    timestamp: number;\n    data: {\n        message?: string;\n        service?: string;\n        tool?: string;\n        args?: Record&lt;string, unknown&gt;;\n        preview?: string;\n        duration?: number;\n        response?: string;\n        status?: string;\n        is_error?: boolean;\n        title?: string;\n        server?: string;\n        progress?: string;\n        servers?: string[];\n        related_services?: string[];\n        [key: string]: unknown;\n    };\n}\n\nexport default function Home() {\n    const [serviceName, setServiceName] = useState('');\n    const [isDiagnosing, setIsDiagnosing] = useState(false);\n    const [report, setReport] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\n    const [runId, setRunId] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\n    const [pipelineState, setPipelineState] = useState&lt;PipelineState&gt;(initialPipelineState);\n    const [events, setEvents] = useState&lt;DiagnosisEvent[]&gt;([]);\n    const [isListening, setIsListening] = useState(false);\n    const [thoughtSteps, setThoughtSteps] = useState&lt;ThoughtStep[]&gt;([]);\n\n    const eventSourceRef = useRef&lt;EventSource | null&gt;(null);\n\n    // Topology-aware state\n    const [faultPropagation, setFaultPropagation] = useState&lt;{ impactPath: string[]; rootCauseService?: string }&gt;({ impactPath: [] });\n    const [serviceGraph, setServiceGraph] = useState&lt;{ services: string[]; edges: Array&lt;{ from: string; to: string }&gt;; healthMap: Record&lt;string, string&gt; }&gt;({\n        services: [],\n        edges: [],\n        healthMap: {}\n    });\n    const [diagnosis, setDiagnosis] = useState&lt;{ rootCause?: string; confidence?: number; evidence?: string[]; runbook?: Array&lt;{ step: number; action: string; command?: string }&gt; }&gt;({});\n\n    // Process events into thought steps and topology data\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        if (events.length &gt; 0) {\n            // Update thought steps\n            setThoughtSteps(eventsToThoughtSteps(events));\n\n            // Continuously update service graph and fault propagation from tool results\n            const serviceGraphData = parseServiceGraph(events);\n            const faultData = parseFaultPropagation(events);\n\n            console.log('[Dashboard] Events count:', events.length);\n            console.log('[Dashboard] Service Graph:', serviceGraphData);\n            console.log('[Dashboard] Fault Propagation:', faultData);\n\n            setServiceGraph(serviceGraphData);\n            setFaultPropagation(faultData);\n\n            // Parse diagnosis data from complete event\n            const completeEvent = events.find(e =&gt; e.event_type === 'complete');\n            if (completeEvent) {\n                console.log('[Dashboard] Complete event found:', completeEvent);\n                console.log('[Dashboard] Response preview:', completeEvent.data.response?.slice(0, 500));\n\n                const diagnosisData = parseDiagnosis(events);\n                console.log('[Dashboard] Parsed diagnosis:', diagnosisData);\n                setDiagnosis(diagnosisData);\n            }\n        }\n    }, [events]);\n\n\n    // Subscribe to SSE events\n    const subscribeToEvents = useCallback((newRunId: string) =&gt; {\n        if (eventSourceRef.current) {\n            eventSourceRef.current.close();\n        }\n\n        setIsListening(true);\n        const eventSource = new EventSource(&#96;${ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/events/${newRunId}&#96;);\n        eventSourceRef.current = eventSource;\n\n        eventSource.onmessage = (e) =&gt; {\n            try {\n                const event: DiagnosisEvent = JSON.parse(e.data);\n                setEvents(prev =&gt; [...prev, event]);\n\n                // Check for completion\n                if (event.event_type === 'complete') {\n                    setIsDiagnosing(false);\n                    setIsListening(false);\n                    eventSource.close();\n\n                    // Set report if we have a response\n                    if (event.data.response) {\n                        setReport(event.data.response);\n                    }\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                console.error('Failed to parse event:', err);\n            }\n        };\n\n        eventSource.onerror = () =&gt; {\n            console.error('EventSource error');\n            setIsListening(false);\n            eventSource.close();\n        };\n    }, []);\n\n    // Update pipeline state when events change\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        if (events.length &gt; 0) {\n            setPipelineState(eventsToPipelineState(events));\n        }\n    }, [events]);\n\n    // Cleanup on unmount\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        return () =&gt; {\n            if (eventSourceRef.current) {\n                eventSourceRef.current.close();\n            }\n        };\n    }, []);\n\n    // Poll for new alert-triggered runs (auto-subscribe to Grafana alerts)\n    useEffect(() =&gt; {\n        let lastSeenRunId: string | null = null;\n\n        const pollForAlerts = async () =&gt; {\n            try {\n                const res = await fetch(&#96;${ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/latest-run&#96;);\n                if (!res.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await res.json();\n\n                // Check if there's a new run we haven't seen\n                if (\n                    data.run_id &amp;&amp;\n                    data.run_id !== lastSeenRunId &amp;&amp;\n                    data.run_id !== runId &amp;&amp;\n                    !isDiagnosing\n                ) {\n                    lastSeenRunId = data.run_id;\n\n                    // Auto-subscribe to the new alert-triggered run\n                    setRunId(data.run_id);\n                    setServiceName(data.service || '');\n                    setIsDiagnosing(true);\n                    setReport(null);\n                    setEvents([]);\n                    setThoughtSteps([]);\n\n                    setPipelineState(initialPipelineState);\n                    // Reset topology state\n                    setFaultPropagation({ impactPath: [] });\n                    setServiceGraph({ services: [], edges: [], healthMap: {} });\n                    setDiagnosis({});\n\n                    subscribeToEvents(data.run_id);\n                }\n            } catch {\n                // Silently ignore polling errors\n            }\n        };\n\n        const interval = setInterval(pollForAlerts, 3000);\n\n        // Initial poll\n        pollForAlerts();\n\n        return () =&gt; clearInterval(interval);\n    }, [runId, isDiagnosing, subscribeToEvents]);\n\n    const handleDiagnose = async (e: React.FormEvent) =&gt; {\n        e.preventDefault();\n        if (!serviceName.trim()) return;\n\n        // Reset state\n        setIsDiagnosing(true);\n        setReport(null);\n        setEvents([]);\n        setThoughtSteps([]);\n\n        setPipelineState(initialPipelineState);\n        setRunId(null);\n        // Reset topology state\n        setFaultPropagation({ impactPath: [] });\n        setServiceGraph({ services: [], edges: [], healthMap: {} });\n        setDiagnosis({});\n\n        try {\n            const response = await fetch(&#96;${ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/diagnose&#96;, {\n                method: 'POST',\n                headers: {\n                    'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',\n                },\n                body: &#96;text=${encodeURIComponent(serviceName)}&#96;,\n            });\n\n            if (!response.ok) {\n                throw new Error('Failed to start diagnosis');\n            }\n\n            const data = await response.json();\n\n            if (data.run_id) {\n                setRunId(data.run_id);\n           &lt;/div&gt;\n                &lt;/header&gt;\n\n                {/* Pipeline Visualization */}\n                &lt;div className=\"mb-8\"&gt;\n                    &lt;h3 className=\"text-xl font-black uppercase mb-4 flex items-center gap-2\"&gt;\n                        &lt;span className=\"bg-black text-white px-2\"&gt;Pipeline&lt;/span&gt; Status\n                    &lt;/h3&gt;\n                    &lt;PipelineFlow state={pipelineState} /&gt;\n                &lt;/div&gt;\n\n                {/* Topology Visualization - Fault Propagation &amp; Service Map */}\n &lt;ServiceDependencyGraph\n                        services={serviceGraph.services}\n                        edges={serviceGraph.edges}\n                        serviceHealthMap={serviceGraph.healthMap}\n                        highlightServices={faultPropagation.impactPath}\n                        isLoading={isDiagnosing &amp;&amp; serviceGraph.services.length === 0}\n                    /&gt;\n                &lt;/div&gt;\n\n                {/* Main Content Grid - Two Columns */}\n                &lt;div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-8\"&gt;\n                    {/* Left Column: Search + Chain of Thought */}\n                    &lt;div className=\"space-y-6\"&gt;\n                        {/* Search Card */}\n                        &lt;div className=\"bg-[#fff1f2] border-4 border-black p-6 shadow-[8px_8px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)]\"&gt;\n                            &lt;h2 className=\"text-lg font-black text-black mb-4 flex items-center gap-2 uppercase transform -rotate-1\"&gt;\n                                &lt;Sparkles className=\"w-5 h-5 text-black\" /&gt;\n                                Start Investigation\n                            &lt;/h2&gt;\n                            &lt;form onSubmit={handleDiagnose} className=\"space-y-3\"&gt;\n                                &lt;div /&gt;\n                                    &lt;input\n                                        type=\"text\"\n                                        value={serviceName}\n _**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Providing Tailwind CSS guidance**

I'm preparing concise instructions to adjust a video container's size using Tailwind classes like w-16 and h-16, specifying exact line ranges and file paths for easy implementation.</details></think>

---

Adjust the logo size in `sre-dashboard/src/app/page.tsx` inside the header block.

The current sizing is set by the container `div` around the video (width/height) and the video itself:

```249:261:sre-dashboard/src/app/page.tsx
<div className="w-16 h-16 bg-white border-4 border-black overflow-hidden shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_#fbbf24]">
  <video
    className="w-full h-full object-cover"
    src="/animated-logo.MP4"
    autoPlay
    loop
    muted
    playsInline
  />
</div>
```

- Change the container dimensions by tweaking `w-16 h-16` (e.g., `w-20 h-20` to enlarge, `w-12 h-12` to shrink).
- Keep `video` at `w-full h-full object-cover` so it fills the container. If you change the container size, the video scales with it.

_**User (2025-12-09 11:50Z)**_

_**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Providing detailed video creation prompt**

I'm creating a concise yet detailed prompt to guide video creation based on the plan, focusing solely on the content without including any code or tool instructions.</details></think>

---

Here’s a ready-to-use, detailed prompt for a video-generation agent (Veo/Kling). It follows the semi-finals story arc, highlights the SRE Agent dashboard visuals (retro black/yellow/pink palette, animated logo, pipeline, chain-of-thought, RCA, metrics), and fits a ~60–90 second cut. You can trim or expand sections by adjusting durations.

---

Title: “AI SRE Agent – From Alert to RCA in Minutes”

Style & tone:
- High-energy, cinematic tech demo; sharp UI close-ups, smooth pans/zooms.
- Color palette: black, white, yellow accents (#fbbf24), pink highlights (#ff9eb5), light blue backgrounds (#e0f2fe).
- UI aesthetic: bold block borders, retro zine vibes, marquee tape, animated logo box.
- Music: confident, modern electronic; subtle rises on key beats.

Narrative beats (visual + on-screen text):
1) Hook (0–5s): Dark background, glowing text overlay: “Every minute of downtime costs $5,600.” Quick flash of a microservices map fading in.
2) Pain (5–12s): Show a stylized ecommerce page glitching; overlay “Checkout latency spike” and “Revenue loss: $93/sec”.
3) Enter SRE Agent (12–20s): Cut to the SRE Dashboard header: animated logo video box, bold black/yellow card. On-screen text: “AI SRE Agent: Autonomous Incident Response”.
4) Pipeline animation (20–30s): Close-up of the pipeline bar: active stage pulsing, animated flow lines; labels like ERROR → LOGS → ALERT → BRAIN → DONE.
5) Live investigation (30–45s): Split-screen: left “Start Investigation” form being triggered; right chain-of-thought feed animating step-by-step (queries, hypotheses, tools). Show SSE-like streaming events.
6) Topology + fault propagation (45–55s): Pan over service dependency graph and fault propagation chain lighting up an impact path; nodes glow red → amber → green as the agent reasons.
7) Hypothesis & RCA (55–70s): Hypothesis panel showing root cause, confidence, evidence bullets; then an RCA report panel with markdown-style sections and code blocks highlighted.
8) Actions & comms (70–80s): Small overlays of Slack/GitHub badges marked “connected”; hint at automated posts/issue creation (no real brand assets, use generic icons).
9) Metrics & impact (80–90s): Metric overlays: “Time to detection: 12s”, “Time to RCA: 1m 45s”, “Cost saved this incident: $336,000”. Final text: “From alert to RCA in minutes.”

Visual details to include:
- Animated logo sits in a 16x16-ish box with a looping video texture; bold black border and drop shadow.
- Header marquee tape: yellow strip with scrolling text like “SYSTEM NORMAL • MONITORING ACTIVE • WAITING FOR INPUT”.
- Buttons and cards: chunky black borders, slight tilt, shadow offsets, uppercase labels.
- Background: subtle grid on a light blue backdrop.
- Chain-of-thought: stacked cards, streaming text, occasional “tool call” badges.
- Fault propagation: path highlights and node pulses; health colors: red (critical), amber (degraded), green (healthy).
- Avoid showing real brands; use generic “chat” and “repo” icons for Slack/GitHub mentions.

Pacing & transitions:
- Quick cuts for the hook/pain, slower pans for the dashboard, gentle zooms on pipeline/graphs.
- Use glow/pulse effects on active stages; data-stream particle hints between boxes.
- End with a confident hold on metrics + tagline.

Aspect ratio & length:
- 16:9, ~60–90 seconds. Clean captions for key callouts; minimal voiceover implied by text overlays.

Key lines for captions/overlays (use sparingly):
- “AI SRE Agent investigating…”
- “Correlating logs, metrics, topology”
- “Root cause identified”
- “Runbook ready”
- “From alert to RCA in minutes”

If you want a longer 12-minute cut, this prompt scales by extending each beat and lingering more on the RCA walkthrough and architecture diagrams, but the above is ideal for a crisp highlight reel.

_**Agent (model gpt-5.1-codex-max, mode Agent)**_



---

Here’s an updated video-agent prompt that contrasts “before chaos” vs “after SRE Agent” while showcasing your dashboard visuals.

Title: “Before vs After: AI SRE Agent”

Style & tone:
- Cinematic tech demo, high contrast. Palette: black/white with yellow (#fbbf24) and pink accents (#ff9eb5); light blue backdrop for the dashboard. Bold, chunky UI borders, slight tilt/shadow, marquee tape.

Narrative structure (~60–90s, 16:9):
1) Before: Chaos (0–15s)
- Visual: Blinking red alerts, terminal noise, disjointed dashboards, engineers scrambling at 3 AM. Overlay text: “Incidents pile up • Root cause unclear • Revenue leaking”.
- Brief glitchy ecommerce checkout failing; counter showing “–$93/sec”.
- Audio: tense, urgent.

2) Before: Slow MTTR (15–25s)
- Visual: Multiple tabs (logs, metrics, traces) with no correlation; stopwatch ticking up; “Time to RCA: hours”.
- Overlay: “Siloed signals. Manual triage. Pager fatigue.”

3) Transition (25–30s)
- Visual: Hard cut to black, then the animated logo box powers on (your video logo), yellow marquee strip scrolling “SYSTEM NORMAL • MONITORING ACTIVE”.

4) After: SRE Agent Takes Over (30–45s)
- Visual: Your SRE Dashboard header with animated logo. Marquee tape. “AI SRE Agent” overlay.
- Pipeline close-up: ERROR → LOGS → ALERT → BRAIN → DONE, active stage pulsing, flow lines animating.

5) After: Live Investigation (45–60s)
- Split-screen: left “Start Investigation” form auto-triggered; right chain-of-thought cards stream hypotheses/tool calls.
- Fault propagation chain lights an impact path; service dependency graph glows red→amber→green as health recovers.

6) After: RCA & Runbook (60–75s)
- Hypothesis panel shows root cause + confidence + evidence bullets.
- RCA report panel with markdown-style sections; code block highlight; “Runbook ready” badge.

7) After: Comms & Automation (75–85s)
- Generic “chat” and “repo” badges marked connected; subtle nod to automated Slack/issue posts (no brand assets).

8) After: Impact (85–95s)
- Metric overlays: “Time to detection: seconds”, “Time to RCA: minutes”, “Cost saved: $336,000”. Calm, resolved sound.

Visual specifics to keep:
- Animated logo in a bordered square, drop shadow.
- Marquee yellow tape with scrolling status.
- Chunky buttons/cards with black borders; light blue grid background.
- Health colors: red/amber/green; pulses on active nodes.
- Particle/glow hints between pipeline stages.

Captions/overlays (sprinkle sparingly):
- Before: “Uncorrelated signals”, “Pager fatigue”, “MTTR: hours”
- After: “AI SRE Agent investigating…”, “Correlating logs/metrics/topology”, “Root cause identified”, “Runbook ready”, “From alert to RCA in minutes”

Pacing:
- Fast, chaotic cuts for “before”; smooth pans/zooms and steady pulses for “after”.

Aspect/length:
- 16:9, target 60–90s. Clean text overlays, minimal voiceover implied. Adjust durations per beat as needed.

---
